epic.name: PagerDuty Schedule & Policy Import Tool
epic.objective: Build a migration tool that imports on-call schedules and escalation policies from PagerDuty via API, creating equivalent OpenIncident configurations with a detailed validation report.
epic.definition_of_done: Users can provide a PagerDuty API key and import their schedules and escalation policies into OpenIncident. The tool generates a report showing what was imported, what was skipped, and any manual adjustments needed.
epic.notes: This is a migration accelerator to reduce friction for PagerDuty users switching to OpenIncident. The tool is a one-time import CLI command, not a continuous sync. PagerDuty API docs: https://developer.pagerduty.com/api-reference/. The import focuses on schedules and escalation policies only; incidents and alerts are NOT imported (those are ephemeral). Users continue operations in OpenIncident post-import.

tasks.name: PagerDuty API client for schedules and policies
tasks.description: Build Go client for PagerDuty REST API to fetch schedules, escalation policies, users, and teams
acceptance_criteria: PagerDutyClient struct with API token authentication (via PAGERDUTY_API_KEY env var)
ListSchedules() → fetch all schedules with pagination (PagerDuty limit 100/page)
GetSchedule(id) → fetch schedule details including layers, rotation times, users
ListEscalationPolicies() → fetch all policies
GetEscalationPolicy(id) → fetch policy details including rules, targets, delays
ListUsers() → fetch all users (for user ID → name/email mapping)
HTTP client with retry logic (exponential backoff) for PagerDuty rate limits (960 req/min)
Error handling: invalid API key, network errors, rate limit exceeded
Unit tests with mock PagerDuty API responses (use httptest.NewServer)
tasks.implementation_notes: PagerDuty API base URL: https://api.pagerduty.com. Authentication: header 'Authorization: Token token={api_key}'. Pagination: include[]=users for schedule details. Response models: PDSchedule, PDLayer, PDEscalationPolicy, PDEscalationRule. Store in internal/integrations/pagerduty/client.go. Retry: 3 attempts with 1s, 2s, 4s backoff. Accept header: 'application/vnd.pagerduty+json;version=2'.

tasks.name: Schedule import logic and mapping
tasks.description: Convert PagerDuty schedule format to OpenIncident schedule models and persist to database
acceptance_criteria: ImportSchedules(pdSchedules) → creates OpenIncident Schedule records with equivalent configuration
Schedule mapping: PD schedule.name → OI name, PD schedule.time_zone → OI timezone, PD schedule.description → OI description
Layer mapping: PD layer.rotation_type (daily/weekly) → OI rotation_type, PD layer.start → OI rotation_start, PD layer.rotation_turn_length_seconds → OI shift_duration_seconds
Participant mapping: PD users[].email → OI participants.user_name (email preferred, fallback to PD user.name)
Handle PagerDuty layer restrictions (start/end dates) → create OpenIncident overrides for gaps
Validation: if PD rotation_type unsupported (e.g., 'custom' with complex rules), skip layer and log warning
Import report: { schedules_imported: N, schedules_skipped: M, layers_imported: X, layers_skipped: Y, warnings: [...] }
Idempotency: if schedule with same name exists, skip (or overwrite with --force flag)
tasks.implementation_notes: PagerDuty rotation_type: 'daily', 'weekly', 'custom'. Only daily/weekly map cleanly. PD 'custom' with handoff_day/handoff_time requires complex logic; skip and warn user. PD layer.users array → OI participants with order_index matching PD order. If PD user has no email, use user.summary (display name). Import report stored in JSON file: pagerduty_import_report.json.

tasks.name: Escalation policy import logic and mapping
tasks.description: Convert PagerDuty escalation policies to OpenIncident format with tier and target mapping
acceptance_criteria: ImportEscalationPolicies(pdPolicies) → creates OpenIncident EscalationPolicy and EscalationTier records
Policy mapping: PD policy.name → OI name, PD policy.description → OI description
Rule mapping: PD escalation_rules (ordered) → OI tiers with tier_index = rule order
Target mapping: PD rule.targets (schedule/user) → OI target_type, schedule_id or user_ids
Delay mapping: PD escalation_delay_in_minutes → OI timeout_seconds (minutes * 60)
If PD rule has multiple targets (e.g., notify schedule AND team), create OI tier with target_type='both'
Validation: if PD target is unsupported (e.g., 'service'), skip and log warning
Import report: { policies_imported: N, policies_skipped: M, tiers_imported: X, warnings: [...] }
Link imported policies to routing rules: create default rule 'Imported from PagerDuty' → imported policy
tasks.implementation_notes: PD escalation_rules[].targets: type='schedule_reference'|'user_reference'|'team_reference'. Map schedule_reference → OI schedule lookup by name (must match imported schedule). Map user_reference → user_ids via email lookup. Map team_reference → skip (OpenIncident has no Teams model yet; deferred to v0.6). If PD policy has repeat_enabled=true, set OI policy to repeat (default behavior).

tasks.name: CLI command for PagerDuty import
tasks.description: Command-line interface for running the PagerDuty import with progress reporting and dry-run mode
acceptance_criteria: CLI command: `openincident import pagerduty --api-key=<key>` or PAGERDUTY_API_KEY env var
Flags: --dry-run (preview import without writing to DB), --force (overwrite existing schedules/policies), --output-report=<path> (default: pagerduty_import_report.json)
Progress reporting: 'Fetching PagerDuty schedules... Found 12 schedules. Importing...' with spinner or progress bar
Import executes in transaction: if any step fails, rollback all changes (atomicity)
Dry-run mode: fetch from PagerDuty, validate mappings, generate report, but do not persist
Report output: JSON file with { summary: { schedules_imported, policies_imported }, warnings: [], errors: [] }
Exit codes: 0 (success), 1 (validation errors), 2 (API errors)
tasks.implementation_notes: CLI uses cobra library (already in project). Command path: cmd/openincident/import_pagerduty.go. Progress: use github.com/briandowns/spinner or simple fmt.Printf. Transaction: wrap import in db.Transaction(). Dry-run: skip db writes, still call validation logic. Report: json.MarshalIndent to file. Document in README.md under 'Migration from PagerDuty'.

tasks.name: Import validation and conflict resolution
tasks.description: Validation logic to detect conflicts, unsupported features, and data quality issues before import
acceptance_criteria: Validate PagerDuty API key: test with GET /users/me, return error if invalid
Detect name conflicts: if OpenIncident schedule/policy with same name exists, log warning and skip (or overwrite with --force)
Validate schedule layers: if PD layer uses unsupported rotation (e.g., hourly, custom complex rules), skip layer and warn
Validate escalation targets: if target references missing schedule or user, skip tier and warn
Check for orphaned references: if PD policy references deleted schedule, warn and skip policy
Report summary includes: 'X of Y schedules imported', 'M schedules skipped due to conflicts', 'N layers skipped (unsupported rotation)'
User-friendly warnings: 'Schedule "Primary On-Call" uses custom rotation with complex rules — skipped. Manual creation required.'
Post-import verification: query imported schedules/policies, compare counts with PagerDuty totals
tasks.implementation_notes: Validation runs before import transaction. Create Validator struct with ValidateSchedule(pdSchedule), ValidateEscalationPolicy(pdPolicy) methods. Collect warnings/errors in []ValidationIssue. If critical errors (e.g., invalid API key, DB connection failure), abort import. If non-critical warnings (e.g., unsupported layer), skip item and continue. Post-import: SELECT COUNT(*) from schedules, escalation_policies, compare with report.

tasks.name: Documentation and import guide
tasks.description: User-facing documentation for the PagerDuty import process with examples and troubleshooting
acceptance_criteria: docs/PAGERDUTY_IMPORT.md created with step-by-step guide
Prerequisites: PagerDuty API key (how to generate), OpenIncident v0.5+ installed
Import steps: 1) Export PagerDuty data, 2) Run dry-run, 3) Review report, 4) Run import, 5) Verify
Example commands: dry-run, force overwrite, custom report path
Troubleshooting section: invalid API key, name conflicts, unsupported rotations, target resolution failures
Manual migration steps for unsupported features (e.g., custom rotation rules)
Link from main README.md under 'Migration' section
Screenshots: import report JSON, OpenIncident UI showing imported schedules
tasks.implementation_notes: Guide targets non-technical users (EM/SRE, not just developers). Emphasize dry-run first. Include sample report JSON with annotations explaining each field. Troubleshooting: 'If rotation_type is custom, manually recreate layer in OpenIncident UI.' Link to PagerDuty API key generation: https://support.pagerduty.com/docs/api-access-keys.

external: Valid PagerDuty API key with read permissions

not_imported: PagerDuty incidents and alerts (ephemeral, not migrated)
PagerDuty services (no OpenIncident equivalent yet; deferred to Service Catalog in v1.x)
PagerDuty integrations (webhooks, email, etc. — user reconfigures in OpenIncident)
Historical escalation logs (only current configuration imported)
partially_supported: Custom rotation rules (complex PD rotations may require manual setup)
Team targets in escalation policies (OpenIncident has no Teams model in v0.5)
